fields.description:
## Why
Two defect classes concern the INVISIBLE AFFECTED SET (existing sites a plan silently breaks). A lexical trigger over-fires on descriptive prose; a code-grounded router is more reliable, and the heavy enumeration deserves its own context window.

## What
- Reusable mechanism: plan_review_G1G2.md (code-grounded) detects the pattern and emits an OVERLAY TRIGGER; orchestrator.py spawns a dedicated agentic overlay in its OWN context window that enumerates existing affected sites and verifies the plan handles each (migrate/exempt/gate). Enumerate consumers OUTSIDE src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ that consume the routing/checklist schema before wiring.
- G-5 instantiation: pattern = "pre-existing code may trigger a NEW prohibition" (enforce/block/require-before). Overlay greps the outlawed behavior's call sites, classifies migrated/exempted/uncovered.
- G-10 instantiation: pattern = new git ref / event source / schedule. Overlay enumerates every workflow trigger filter (INCLUDED/EXCLUDED/NO_FILTER) + release-infra coverage. Abstain-with-coverage on unparseable CI (polyglot fail-open).

## Provenance
Labels like G-8 / FP-3a / R-6 / WS-N are provenance tags from the DSO gap-report evaluation (~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md) + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020) -- they are NOT codebase symbols and cannot be grepped. Concrete code targets are named inline and in this ticket's file_impact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Router->overlay mechanism: G1G2 emits a trigger; overlay runs as a separate pass. Proof: unit test asserting a prohibition-shaped plan spawns the overlay; `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k overlay`.
- [ ] G-5: recall case where a require-tests-before-merge plan yields uncovered `gh pr merge` call sites.
- [ ] G-10: recall case where a new ref pattern yields an EXCLUDED workflow; fail-open abstain case on unknown CI.
- [ ] G4 consumer-enumeration reuses the same worklist idiom (cross-ref WS5).
